summary: Replaced progress.yaml writes with append-only summary.jsonl, updated summary loading in runner/app paths, and added JSONL regression coverage. Verification passed: go test ./..., go build ./..., strict OpenSpec validate, diff check, and temp jq acceptance.

Added commitSetupFiles helper to cmd/rally/main.go. After rally init, stages .rally/.gitignore, .rally/config.toml, .rally/README.md and commits as 'rally: initialize workspace'. After laps.InstallHooks, stages .laps/hooks.json and hook scripts and commits as 'rally: install laps hooks'. Both use --no-verify, skip when nothing staged, never sweep user changes, and warn gracefully on git failures. go build and go test pass. Smoke-tested: first init creates exactly one setup commit, second is no-op, dirty working tree files are not swept.

Added 5 tests for commitSetupFiles in cmd/rally/main_test.go covering: init creates exactly one 'rally: initialize workspace' commit, init rerun is no-op, dirty working tree user files are not swept, hook install creates exactly one 'rally: install laps hooks' commit, and hook reinstall is no-op. All use real git repos via gitx helpers. Concurrent-rally duplicate-commit coverage is best-effort — the sequential tests prove idempotency for single-process flows; a true concurrent race would need goroutine-based stress testing with serialization points that are inherently non-deterministic.

Added lap-boundary commit instructions to both hook scripts. laps-done-hook.sh now instructs agent to commit with '<lap-description>: done'. laps-handoff-hook.sh now instructs agent to commit with '<lap-description>: in progress (handoff)'. Both include guidance to replace the placeholder. Added assertions in hooks_test.go for commit message forms and placeholder guidance. All go test ./internal/laps/... tests pass.

I wonder if maybe the problem is the `store.NewStore` creates the `.rally/state/` directory, and git doesn't show `.rally/` as a single entry anymore because there's a gitignored subdir. Let me try:

Leftover-work commit guidance at run start: dirty non-rally tree injects advisory commit-first prompt via BuildPromptOptions.LeftoverWork + embedded leftover_work.md snippet; tolerates IsWorkspaceDirty errors. Also extended IsWorkspaceDirty to exclude .laps/ (rally's own queue/hook machinery) so guidance doesn't spuriously fire every run. Tests added for dirty/clean/only-rally cases; full suite green.

Verified phases 1-2 of git-hygiene: all 11 targeted tasks (1.1-1.4, 2.1-2.5, 4.3, 4.4) pass deep review. Setup auto-commit uses --no-verify, staged-set guard, and path-scoped git add/commit (no -A). Hook scripts emit exact message forms with placeholder guidance. Leftover-work guidance toggles on IsWorkspaceDirty (now also excludes .laps/), threaded via BuildPromptOptions.LeftoverWork -> embedded leftover_work.md. All 11 tests pass. No substantive gaps found; no follow-up laps needed. Tasks 3.x, 4.1-4.2, 4.5-4.7 remain for later phases.

Phase 3 git-hygiene: retired CommitRallyState/rallyTrackedStatePaths/pathIsGitIgnored; added gitx.FoldRallyState (insurance fold for no-code runs — stages only .rally/.laps, amends rally-prefixed HEAD with [+state] preserving authorship or creates single 'rally: update state', no stacking); rewired runner.go:1133 call site; removed obsolete tests. go build/vet/test green, grep clean.

Phase 3 task 3.5: added 5 tests in internal/gitx/git_test.go covering FoldRallyState's finalization commit behavior over temp git repos — code-run (one work commit folds summary.jsonl, no separate state commit), no-code amend of rally-prefixed HEAD (+[+state] once, authorship preserved, no stacking), no-code non-rally HEAD (single 'rally: update state', second fold amends not stacks), no-changes no-op, and non-git no-op. Drove the real finalization helper (runner.go:1137 calls FoldRallyState); modeled autoCommit's 'git add -A' work commit via a commitAll helper. go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/gitx/... and go vet pass.

Phase 3 verified: all 5 tasks (3.1-3.5) confirmed complete and correct. FoldRallyState replaces CommitRallyState; autoCommit folds summary.jsonl into work commit; amend-fallback handles no-code runs. Zero dangling references. 5/5 tests pass + smoke confirmed. go build/vet clean. No gaps — no follow-up laps.

Added 15 edge-case hardening tests covering special characters in commit messages (quotes, newlines, dollar signs, backticks) across gitx, cmd/rally, and laps hook scripts; plus git-unavailable/corrupted scenarios (non-git dir, empty .git). All tests pass, go vet clean. No production bugs found — exec.Command passes args directly to git without shell interpolation.
